evidence-binding: n1-claim-677 ecc1af30b4ff
<!-- RED: paste RED here -->
RED: added #677a (cmdStaleWorktreeCleanup unprobeable-keep, mirrored from #672) and #677b (parent-unreadable regression, chmod 000 legacy container) to scripts/test-claim-hardening.js and ran `node scripts/test-claim-hardening.js` against pre-fix worktreeDirtyState. #677a passed immediately (pure coverage gap, no defect on that path). #677b failed exactly as predicted with 3 assertion failures: "FAIL: #677b: removed must NOT include a worktree whose parent dir was merely unreadable (existsSync false != missing), got [\".../kw-677b-repo-WKMN99.kw/issue-96774\"]"; "FAIL: #677b: skipped_unprobeable must record the parent-unreadable worktree (fail LOUD, not silent), got {\"removed\":[\".../issue-96774\"],...,\"skipped_unprobeable\":[]}"; "FAIL: #677b: the worktree registration must SURVIVE cleanup --execute (kept, not pruned) once access is restored" — final line "claim-hardening tests FAILED (3 failures, 182 passed)", proving the A2 fail-open (existsSync false-on-unreadable-parent -> misclassified 'missing' -> pruned) pre-fix.
<!-- GREEN: paste GREEN here -->
GREEN: applied the fix — worktreeDirtyState (scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js, mirrored byte-identically into plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js via `edition-sync --write`, and hand-ported into plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/kaola-gitlab-workflow-claim.js + plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/kaola-gitea-workflow-claim.js) now opens with `fs.lstatSync(wtPath)` in try/catch: ENOENT -> 'missing', any other error (EACCES/ENOTDIR on an unreadable parent) -> 'unprobeable' (reuses the existing #672 keep state, no new state). Re-ran `node scripts/test-claim-hardening.js` -> "claim-hardening tests passed (185 assertions)" (182 baseline + 3 new #677b assertions now green, plus #677a's assertions already counted). `node scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` -> exit 0, last line "Workflow walkthrough simulation passed" (19/19 sub-tests PASSED). `node scripts/edition-sync.js --check` -> "edition-sync: 10 forge aggregator ports, 24 COMMON_SCRIPTS mirrors, and 27 byte-identical groups in parity with canonical." (clean, canonical/codex still byte-identical after --write regen).
